hinge: Early RPD failures commonly involve clasp problems — deformation, fracture, loss of retention, or tissue trauma from ill-fitting clasps. Connectors and bases fail too, but clasps are the most frequent early mechanical complaint.

Microfilled composites polish well but have lower fracture resistance for load-bearing areas. Modern nanofilled/nanohybrid composites combine polishability with improved mechanical properties for redoing composite veneers.

Recession limited to buccal surfaces without interproximal attachment loss is a mucogingival deformity/condition of buccal recession (Cairo RT1), not interproximal recession and not automatically periodontitis staging.

Tranexamic acid is an antifibrinolytic used to reduce bleeding in hemophilia and other bleeding disorders, systemically or as a topical oral rinse. It is not indicated for stroke, DVT, or valve disease.
A severely autistic adult who lacks decision-making capacity cannot give valid informed consent. The legally authorized surrogate (typically the parent/guardian) provides consent.

Cobalt-chromium has a much higher modulus of elasticity (stiffness) than gold alloys. Thin major connectors and bases resist flexure and fracture better under functional load due to this high stiffness.

Cusp tip reduction is indicated when the same cusp participates in both centric and eccentric interferences. Selective grinding hierarchy dictates cusp tip reduction rather than fossa or incline-only adjustments when both contact types exist.

At denture insertion, first verify the base fit, adaptation, and pressure areas. Checking occlusion before confirming the denture seats correctly misattributes errors. Fit is the first check before borders and occlusion.

Orthopnea requiring multiple pillows plus dependent leg swelling is classic congestive heart failure from fluid overload and elevated filling pressures. Diabetes alone does not produce this orthopnea-edema cluster.
A beefy red atrophic tongue often reflects nutritional deficiency (B vitamins, iron). Management centers on improving nutrition and correcting the deficiency after appropriate laboratory workup.

Gustatory sweating and pain while eating is Frey syndrome — auriculotemporal nerve misdirection where parasympathetic fibers reinnervate sweat glands instead of the parotid, typically following parotid surgery or trauma.
Target or bull's-eye concentric-ring skin lesions are the hallmark of erythema multiforme. Sebaceous glands and mucoepidermoid carcinoma do not produce this cutaneous target morphology.
Powder dentifrices are typically more abrasive than gel or paste formulations and are associated with greater dentin and cementum abrasion when used aggressively during toothbrushing.

Celluloid strip crowns are used in pediatric dentistry to restore primary anterior teeth after caries or trauma, including incisal fractures, when full-coverage esthetic form is needed with composite resin.

Post-MI patients allergic to acetaminophen need an analgesic with the lowest cardiovascular risk profile. Among common NSAIDs, naproxen has a more favorable cardiovascular safety record compared to diclofenac or high-dose ibuprofen based on current evidence.
Pilocarpine is a cholinergic sialogogue indicated for xerostomia when residual salivary function exists, such as in Sjögren syndrome or post-radiation dry mouth.

Wheezing and dyspnea developing during a procedure using a rubber dam specifically points to latex allergy (Type I IgE-mediated hypersensitivity). The rubber dam is the most direct and testable cause-effect relationship for acute respiratory distress in this context.

For an avulsed permanent incisor with approximately 45 minutes extraoral time and closed apex: replant with a flexible (non-rigid) splint. Pulp necrosis is inevitable with this extraoral duration, so start root canal treatment within 7-10 days with calcium hydroxide intracanal medication.

Mandibular expansion with an orange peel or ground-glass radiographic appearance is classic for fibrous dysplasia. Periapical and florid cemento-osseous dysplasias are typically non-expansile; ossifying fibroma is a well-demarcated neoplasm.

In a young child, open bite and unilateral crossbite from thumb-sucking often improve spontaneously if the habit stops early. Many protocols delay interceptive habit appliances until the later mixed dentition (~7-8 years) when the child is more cooperative and the habit may have self-resolved.

Insufficient keratinized gingiva in the mandibular anterior region often coexists with a high frenum pull. A free gingival graft augments keratinized tissue width while combining with frenectomy addresses the frenal pull. CTG mainly provides root coverage/thickness rather than pure KG width.

A midline diastema appearing during rapid maxillary expansion is expected as the midpalatal suture opens. Continue planned activations — it is not an emergency and the space will close spontaneously as the suture stabilizes.

The quad helix expands the maxilla to correct posterior crossbite and can be modified with crib elements to address the thumb-sucking habit simultaneously. It addresses the transverse discrepancy better than a pure palatal crib, which only targets the habit.

A master apical file size #55 corresponds to an ISO #55 gutta-percha master cone with standard 0.02 taper. A 0.04 taper cone would match a correspondingly enlarged tapered preparation, not a standard #55 hand-file preparation.

Maxillary lateral incisor (#12) with necrotic pulp, deep palatal probing, and minimal plaque is classic for dens invaginatus allowing bacterial ingress directly into the pulp through the developmental defect.

Gingival enlargement classified by extent: papillary (limited to interdental papillae), marginal (gingival margin zone), or diffuse (extends to attached gingiva and vestibular fold). Inflammation reaching the vestibular fold is diffuse enlargement.

Internal inflammatory root resorption appears radiographically as a radiolucent expansion of the pulp chamber or canal outline that is continuous with the pulp cavity. It is not a peripheral radiopacity around the root.

Emergency dental treatment may proceed after recent MI with the requested medical precautions (BP monitoring). Clopidogrel is an antiplatelet agent — do not order INR (for warfarin only). Routine antibiotic prophylaxis is not indicated solely for MI/antiplatelet therapy, and deferring true emergency care could be more harmful.

Insufficient occlusal reduction for a PFM crown leaves inadequate space for proper porcelain thickness. The restoration will either be overcontoured causing occlusal dysfunction and high contacts, or have porcelain too thin risking fracture. Occlusal dysfunction from inadequate reduction space is the primary clinical consequence.

Class V cervical facial/lingual restorations generally do not require a matrix. Contour and isolation are managed with hand instruments. Tofflemire and sectional matrices are designed for proximal boxes, not cervical lesions.
